title: Supply Chain & Logistics Business Unit Analysis
sources_used: Daily Cash Fcst - 5.26.23_BU view_24 adj_MayBOD_Liquidity & May actuals.xlsx
Daily Cash Fcst - 12.10.24_BU view_deck version_BOD_SC wo 3 RE Interco IF_$9M.xlsx

executive_summary.business_description: Supply Chain (SC) / 3PL is Transform Holdco's third-party logistics unit, operating distribution centers (DCs) under the Innovel/Supply Chain brand. The unit provides warehousing, fulfillment, parcel delivery (via UPS, FedEx), ocean freight (Tetria Global Logistics, Crowley), truckload/LTL carrier management (Ultra, Intermodal Sales, Echo Global), and offshore (Hawaii/Puerto Rico) shipping. The business evolved from the legacy Sears/Kmart logistics infrastructure into a third-party revenue-generating 3PL business.
executive_summary.scale_at_peak: BU beta tabs show SC inflows of $4M–$8M/month in FY22 (Sept 2022 BOD: $4.74M–$8.49M/month, implied FY22 run-rate $59.3M/yr per internal 'zCF' annotation: 'Supply Chain $82M/yr'). SC disbursements (logistics costs: carriers, DC operations) averaged $2.0M–$2.9M/month in FY22.
executive_summary.3PL_note: The $82M/yr reference on BU beta includes intercompany SC services to Home Services, Retail, and KCD divisions. True external 3PL revenue (from third parties) was tracked separately in the 'SC IF Forecast' tabs, totaling ~$1.9M–$2.6M/month ($23–31M/yr) by 2025. This is the commercially distinct 3PL revenue stream.

sc_inflows_monthly_by_BOD.note: Inflows Detail tab column 14 = Supply Chain cash inflows per month. BU beta inflows (row 17-18) represent the broader SC inflow line including intercompany. All figures $M.

Sep2022_BOD.note: BU beta FY22 total SC disbursements: $26.92M. Internal annotation: 'Bruce has a 3PL Tracker that logs all invoices and payments'

Jan2024_BOD.note: Step down to ~$3M/month range as retail operations wound down

Dec2024_BOD.note: This file title indicates SC inflows EXCLUDING 3 Real Estate Intercompany items totaling $9M. See dec_2024_9m_re_interco section for details.

Mar2025_BOD.note: Uptick to $4M/month - new 3PL clients being added

sc_disbursements_logistics_costs.note: Logistics disbursements tracked in 'logistics details'/'SC detail'/'Logistics XXXX' tabs. All figures $M. These are cash outflows for carrier payments.

carrier_categories.Ocean_Freight: Tetria Global Logistics (primary ocean), UPS Supply Chain Solutions (backup)
carrier_categories.Offshore: Crowley Liner Services (Puerto Rico/Hawaii primary), Pacific Transportation Lines (Hawaii), Island Delivery Service
carrier_categories.TL_Carriers: Ultra Logistics, Coyote Logistics, Intermodal Sales, Heartland Express, Swift Transportation, AFN/GlobalTranz
carrier_categories.LTL: Echo Global Logistics, TForce (fka UPS Freight), CH Robinson
carrier_categories.DC_Operations: Penske Truck Leasing (equipment), Brightview Landscape (DC maintenance), various DC construction vendors

total_monthly_transport_actuals.note: Transportation costs allocated primarily to HS (96%), Retail (2-4%), OBU (2-3%), KCD (1%) per BU allocation columns. SC/3PL column typically 0% for carrier costs — carriers bill intercompany through HS/Retail cost centers.

May2026_6month_view.FY26_est: declining to $3-5M/month by mid-2026 as operations wind down

three_pl_external_revenue_by_dc.note: From SC IF Forecast tabs (Sep 2024, Dec 2024, Mar 2025, Jul 2025). These track third-party customer revenue — NOT intercompany SC services. All figures monthly $, converted to $M for summary.

dec_2024_9m_re_interco_analysis.file: Daily Cash Fcst - 12.10.24_BU view_deck version_BOD_SC wo 3 RE Interco IF_$9M.xlsx
dec_2024_9m_re_interco_analysis.title_meaning: This BOD file presents the Supply Chain cash flow EXCLUDING 3 Real Estate intercompany items totaling $9M. This was created to show the 'pure' SC/3PL cash contribution without inflating it with non-3PL intercompany RE transactions.

items.description: Parking revenue from Amazon and Berry Plastics at Fairless Hills DC. Was booked to Real Estate BU but SC team questioned: 'currently booked to Real Estate - why?' Should flow through SC/3PL P&L.
True inflows upside — revised baseline SC revenue forecast not reflected in the Oct 2024 version of the CF model.

3PL invoices from SC to Brands/KCD divisions. Home Services intercompany was already eliminated, but Brands/KCD were not being invoiced. '3PL invoices to Brands - revenue not currently in this version of the CF.'

the_3_RE_interco_items_identified.note: The file title references '$9M' but the SC Interco sheet shows total net CF change of $4.39M (FY24). The $9M likely refers to the gross revenue upside including TBD items or refers to a prior version. The 3 identified RE interco items sum to ~$5.5M annually. Additional intercompany items include: Retail Services ($3.096M/yr — 3PL expenses for Retail not being invoiced back).

interco_items_deducted_from_46M_baseline.description: Items removed from $46M SC inflow baseline to get true external 3PL number

propco_arrangement.description: Transform pays PropCo (Holdco's real estate entity / property company) monthly per a reconciliation file. The PropCo relationship for Supply Chain specifically covers: (1) 3PL EBITDA sharing for Lawrence KS and Morrisville facilities, (2) Management fees. This is a monthly outflow from Transform to PropCo.

cash_flow_line.label: Propco
cash_flow_line.description_from_cash_file: Monthly recon file with Propco (for 3PL EBITDA (for Lawrence & Morrisville), mgmt fees, etc)

propco_2025_line.label: PropCo 2025
propco_2025_line.description: Midco paying PH25 for property taxes for UBS properties accrued prior to FY25. Should only be mgmt fees in FY26.

propco_arrangement.interpretation: Transform operates the distribution centers at Lawrence KS and Morrisville (PA/NJ) under an arrangement where PropCo owns the real estate. Transform collects the 3PL EBITDA (operating profit from warehousing/3PL services) and then remits a portion to PropCo via monthly reconciliation. This is not a pure lease — it's a profit-sharing or management fee arrangement specifically tied to 3PL EBITDA performance. The FY26 forecast of $12M/yr in Propco outflows suggests this is a significant ongoing obligation.
propco_arrangement.note_on_morrisville: Morrisville DC construction items appeared in Mar 2025 Logistics tab: AKON Curtains LLC and Gallen Engineering for Morrisville construction — indicating active capital investment in this DC during 2025.

longitudinal_sc_inflow_trend_M.note: Monthly SC inflows from Inflows Detail (col 14). These are actual cash deposits tagged SC.
longitudinal_sc_inflow_trend_M.trend_narrative: SC cash inflows from Inflows Detail peaked at $5–6M/month during FY23 (mid-2023), stepped down to $3–4M/month in early-mid FY24 as B&M retail wound down, then settled at $2–2.6M/month steady-state through late FY24 and into FY25 (pure 3PL mode). An uptick to $3.3–4M in Apr-Jun 2025 reflects new client wins (Franchise Group, AFNewCo, Uber Freight Sort Center, Church & Dwight). By late FY25 and FY26, inflows appear to reset to $2M range, possibly reflecting client churn or seasonal patterns.
longitudinal_sc_inflow_trend_M.note_on_monthly_starts: Month-start SC values (SEP, DEC, MAR, JUL BOD cutoff months) show near-zero because those are the first day(s) of the new BOD period with no deposits yet. The sub-$1M months (Jun 2024, Jan 2024, Dec 2024, Jul 2025, Dec 2025) are partial-month actuals at BOD date, not full-month.

net_sc_contribution_to_cash_flow.note: Net = SC inflows (3PL cash received) minus SC disbursements (BU beta allocation). FY22 data most complete.

FY22_annual.note: FY22 net contribution $32.4M before overhead allocations. BU beta data.

FY25_3PL_only.note: From SC IF Forecast Grand Total FY25. Does not include intercompany HS revenue.
